title: CHO-K1 maintenance (ATCC)
description: Adherent CHO-K1 (ATCC CCL-61) in F-12K + 10% FBS, split 1:4 to 1:8. Source: ATCC https://www.atcc.org/products/ccl-61

payload.description: Routine maintenance of adherent CHO-K1 Chinese hamster ovary cells per the ATCC CCL-61 product sheet. Complete growth medium: ATCC-formulated F-12K Medium (ATCC 30-2004) + 10% FBS. Subcultivation ratio 1:4 to 1:8. Rinse with D-PBS, then dissociate with Trypsin-EDTA (ATCC 30-2101), 5 to 15 min. 37 C, 5% CO2. ECACC additionally advises passaging sub-confluent (70 to 80%) and seeding at 1 to 2 x 10^4 cells/cm^2. Doubling time is not published on these sheets, so it is omitted. Sources: ATCC https://www.atcc.org/products/ccl-61 ; ECACC https://www.culturecollections.org.uk/nop/product/cho-k1?refId=85051005&collection=ecacc_gc

cell_line.name: CHO-K1
cell_line.notes: ATCC CCL-61. Grow at 37 C, 5% CO2. Serum is fetal bovine serum (ATCC 30-2020) at 10%. Source: https://www.atcc.org/products/ccl-61

media.base_medium: F-12K Medium (Kaighn's modification of Ham's F-12), ATCC 30-2004

planned_events.notes: Seed day. Rinse with D-PBS, add 2 to 3 mL Trypsin-EDTA (ATCC 30-2101), disperse 5 to 15 min, quench, count, and reseed (ECACC: 1 to 2 x 10^4 cells/cm^2).
Subculture sub-confluent (ECACC: 70 to 80%). Repeat the trypsinize and reseed.
